reset_tests.description: Synchronous reset load: drive a nonzero evolving state first, then assert rst=1 with reinit=0 and advance=0 at a rising edge; verify state loads initial_state exactly on that posedge, not before, and out immediately matches initial_state[0].

vectors.precondition: Reach state different from initial_state, for example initial_state=5'b10110, taps=5'b10010, advance for 1-2 cycles with rst=0 and reinit=0.

vectors.timing_check: Hold rst high only between edges first; verify no state change until posedge. This separates synchronous-reset behavior from incorrect asynchronous-reset mutants.

reset_tests.description: Reset priority over all other controls: assert rst=1 with reinit=1 and advance=1 at a rising edge; verify state still loads initial_state and does not advance.

vectors.precondition: Current state intentionally not equal to initial_state.

reset_tests.description: Reset reload with varied seeds including zero, all-ones, alternating, and single-hot values; verify reset always loads exact initial_state, with no dependence on taps.

normal_tests.description: Basic advance operation with explicit oracle. Use state=10110 and taps=10010 so feedback = ^(10110 & 10010)=^(10010)=1, therefore next_state must be 01101 and out must be 1.
Hold behavior: with rst=0, reinit=0, advance=0, state must remain unchanged across multiple rising edges and outputs must stay stable.
Reinit operation: from an arbitrary nonmatching current state, assert reinit=1 with rst=0 and advance=0; verify state reloads initial_state exactly.

vectors.precondition: Current state is not equal to initial_state, for example reach 01101 from prior advance.

normal_tests.description: Output consistency: after each state-changing edge, verify out always equals out_state[0] in the same cycle observation and does not lag by one clock.

vectors.expect_relation: out == out_state[0] and both hold constant

corner_case_tests.description: Priority corner case: reinit and advance asserted together with rst=0. Verify reinit wins and state loads initial_state instead of shifting.

Zero taps case: with taps=00000, feedback must always be 0. Starting from a nonzero seed, repeated advances must shift toward zero with exact trace 11111 -> 11110 -> 11100 -> 11000 -> 10000 -> 00000 -> 00000.
Zero initial_state absorbing case: load 00000, then advance with several different taps patterns; verify state remains 00000 for all advances.

Shift-direction discriminator: choose a state whose correct MSB-shift result differs strongly from LSB-shift or feedback-in-MSB mutants. Example current_state=10001, taps=00001 gives feedback=1, so correct next_state=00011.

Tap-alignment and reduction discriminator: choose taps and state so XOR of masked bits is 1 while OR, XNOR, or XOR of wrong slice differ. Example current_state=10101, taps=00111 gives masked bits 00101 and feedback=0.
Timing edge case for synchronous controls: toggle rst or reinit between rising edges and verify no update occurs until the next posedge; also verify no negedge-triggered behavior.

between_edges.rst: pulse high then low without a rising clk edge

between_edges.reinit: pulse high then low around falling edge only

corner_case_tests.description: Boundary patterns: seeds/taps of all-zeros, all-ones, alternating 10101/01010, and single-hot taps to expose wrong bit indexing and stale-output behavior.

vectors.expectation_rule: For each advance, compute next_state = {state[3], state[2], state[1], state[0], ^(state & taps)} and check out == next_state[0] after the edge.

sequence_tests.description: Multi-cycle golden trace with fixed taps to expose off-by-one and stale-output bugs. Start from initial_state=10110, taps=10010. Expected states after successive advance edges: 01101, 11011, 10111, 01110, 11101. Check out each cycle as LSB of listed state.

Interleaved hold/advance/reinit sequence: load seed, advance once, hold twice, advance once, reinit, hold once, advance once. This distinguishes unconditional-advance mutants and one-cycle-late update bugs.

Priority sequence: after loading a known seed, apply advance, then a cycle with reinit=1 and advance=1, then another advance. Expected trace proves reinit overrides advance only on its asserted cycle.

Control-edge timing sequence: change advance shortly after a posedge so it should only affect the next posedge; then change it around a falling edge to confirm no negedge sensitivity. Expected state trace must align exactly with rising-edge sampling only.
sequence_tests.sequence_rule: Use a known seed and taps; sample outputs immediately after each posedge and verify state changes only on edges where control was stable high at that posedge.

random_tests.description: Generate constrained-random multi-cycle tests over initial_state, taps, and controls. Each trial should begin by loading a random initial_state using rst or reinit, then run 5-15 cycles with random rst/reinit/advance values, biased toward simultaneous assertions and control toggles near non-sampling times. Use an independent cycle-accurate oracle: if rst then state=initial_state; else if reinit then state=initial_state; else if advance then state={state[3:0], ^(state & taps)} interpreted as new_state[4:1]=old_state[3:0], new_state[0]=feedback; else hold. Check after every posedge that out_state equals oracle state and out equals oracle state[0]. Include high weight on taps=00000, taps=11111, initial_state=00000, initial_state=11111, alternating patterns, and repeated hold cycles.

key_discriminators: Reset is synchronous active-high only; asserting rst between rising edges must not change state until the next posedge.
Priority must be rst > reinit > advance > hold.
When reinit and advance are both high on a rising edge, state must load initial_state rather than advance.
Advance uses current state, not next state, to compute feedback.
Correct next-state formula is new_state[4:1]=current_state[3:0] and new_state[0]=^(current_state & taps).
Shift direction is toward MSB side; current_state[4] is discarded.
Feedback bit is inserted into LSB, not MSB.
Feedback must be XOR reduction of masked bits only; wrong tap alignment, OR/XNOR, or full-state XOR should fail targeted vectors.
With advance=0 and rst=reinit=0, state must hold exactly across all rising edges.
With taps=00000, repeated advances must drive any state toward 00000 by zero-filling the LSB feedback.
With initial_state=00000, all subsequent states remain 00000 for any taps.
out must always equal out_state[0] and must not lag by one cycle or reflect a different state bit.
Outputs must reflect the updated registered state immediately after the state-changing posedge, not one cycle later.
No behavior should occur on falling edges or due to asynchronous sensitivity to rst/reinit.
